name: PySide6 (Qt6 Bindings for Python — Official)
pricing_notes: LGPLv3 (open source) or commercial Qt license; The Qt Company

performance.notes: Native Qt rendering; OpenGL via QOpenGLWidget; hardware-accelerated

agent_readiness.notes: Official Qt6 Python bindings by The Qt Company. from PySide6.QtWidgets import QApplication, QWidget, QPushButton. app = QApplication(). window = QWidget(). button = QPushButton('Click me', window). window.show(). app.exec(). Qt Designer .ui files: QUiLoader. Signals/slots: button.clicked.connect(handler). PyQt6 is third-party alternative (GPLv3 or commercial). Model/View architecture. OpenGL, WebEngine, Multimedia, Network, SQL, Charts modules. QML for modern UI. PySide6 vs PyQt6: PySide6 is official, PyQt6 is commercial GPLv3.

gotchas: LGPLv3 requires dynamic linking or commercial license for commercial products
Large package size (~100MB+ for full Qt)
C++ Qt documentation translates to Python but requires mental mapping
Qt Designer .ui files need compilation step (uic) or runtime loading
best_when: Complex desktop applications requiring full Qt widget set
Scientific instrument UIs with real-time plotting (pyqtgraph)
Applications requiring Qt Designer visual layout
avoid_when: When GPL/LGPL license conflicts with commercial product requirements
summary: PySide6 provides official Qt6 Python bindings by The Qt Company. QApplication + QWidget + QPushButton with signals/slots. Qt Designer .ui files. Full Qt module access: Widgets, Charts, WebEngine, Multimedia, Network, SQL. QML for modern UI. LGPLv3 or commercial license. ~100MB package. PyQt6 is the third-party alternative (GPLv3 or commercial). Most powerful Python desktop GUI — use for complex applications requiring full Qt widget set.
